pages.text: Aging is the slow refinement of cured tobacco over time. Unlike active fermentation, aging is usually gentler. It allows aromas to settle, harsh notes to fade, and the leaf to become more balanced.

Good aging conditions should be stable and moderate. Extreme heat, excessive moisture, or constant disturbance can ruin tobacco that would otherwise improve slowly. Aging should reward patience rather than force.
In gameplay terms, aging is a strong way to add value to already decent tobacco. It should not instantly rescue bad leaf, but it can turn competent leaf into something much more refined.
